description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

surface_forms: America is the hope of all the young people
description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

surface_forms: Japanese who were limited to small islands
description: island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: volcano in Yamanashi and Shizuoka Prefectures, Japan
description_wiki: Mount Fuji (富士山・富士の山, Fujisan, Fuji no Yama) is an active stratovolcano located on the Japanese island of Honshu, with a summit elevation of 3,776.24 m (12,389 ft 3 in).

description: county seat of San Joaquin County, California, United States
description_wiki: Stockton is a city in and the county seat of San Joaquin County, California, United States.

description: city and county seat in Yuba County, California, United States
description_wiki: Marysville is a city in and the county seat of Yuba County, California, located in the Gold Country region of Northern California.

description: second generation of Japanese immigrants
description_wiki: Nisei (二世, "second generation") is a Japanese-language term used in countries in North America and South America to specify the ethnically Japanese children born in the new country to Japanese-born immigrants, or Issei.

description: Japanese people who were the first generation of immigrants from Japan
description_wiki: Issei (一世, "first generation") are Japanese immigrants to countries in North America and South America.

description: grandchildren of Japanese-born emigrants
description_wiki: Sansei (三世, "third generation") is a Japanese and North American English term used in parts of the world (mainly in South America and North America) to refer to the children of children born to ethnically Japanese emigrants (Issei) in a new country of residence, outside of Japan.

description: designated city in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Yokohama (Japanese: 横浜; pronounced [jokohama] ) is the second-largest city in Japan by population as well as by area, and the country's most populous municipality.

aliases: Independence Day in the United States
description: United States holiday held on July 4 to mark the Declaration of Independence
description_wiki: Independence Day, known colloquially as the Fourth of July, is a federal holiday in the United States which commemorates the adoption of the Declaration of Independence on July 4, 1776, establishing the United States of America.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: seat of Multnomah County, and largest city in state of Oregon, United States
description_wiki: Portland ( PORT-lənd) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Oregon.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Kumamoto Prefecture (熊本県, Kumamoto-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [kɯ.ma.mo.to, -toꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located on the island of Kyūshū.

description: period of Japanese history from 1912 to 1926, under the reign of Emperor Taishō

surface_forms: a law was passed that Japanese could not come to America
description: 1924 United States anti-immigration law
description_wiki: The Immigration Act of 1924, also known as the Johnson–Reed Act, or National Origins Act (Pub.

description: capital and largest city of Japan
description_wiki: Tokyo, officially the Tokyo Metropolis, is the capital and most populous city of Japan.

description: one of 8 regions in Japan encompassing 8 prefectures
description_wiki: The Kantō region (関東地方, Kantō Chihō; IPA: [kaꜜn.toː, kan.toː tɕiꜜ.hoː, kan.toː tɕi̥.hoꜜː]) is a geographical region of Honshu, the largest island of Japan.

description: ethnic group; Americans of Japanese ancestry
description_wiki: Japanese Americans (Japanese: 日系アメリカ人) are Americans of Japanese ancestry.
